Factories.description: Manufacturing site entity (ISO §4.1 organisational context) with business segment classification: LPT (Large Power Transformers), MT (Medium Transformers), or DT (Distribution Transformers).

attributes.name: forecasts
attributes.notes: All forecast records belonging to this factory

Report-A.rule: Count of Factories grouped by region

Report-B.query: All factories with computed columns: active forecast count, open ticket count, active project count, region

Forecasts.description: A Forecast (ISO §6.1) groups a set of Forecast Scopes so that a Factory can project to global engineering its intended use of role resources for a given planning period.

attributes.name: forecastScopes
attributes.notes: All child Forecast Scope records belonging to this forecast

attributes.name: totalEstimatedHours
attributes.notes: Total demand projected across all scopes

Report-A.rule: totalEstimatedHours plotted by period (year + quarter), one series per factory

Forecast Scopes.description: The atomic unit of a Forecast (ISO §6.1/§6.2). Specifies one Event × Product Scope combination; the Event determines the triggered Process and therefore the Tasks whose roles and execution times drive estimatedHours.

attributes.name: eventID
attributes.notes: Event that triggers the Process; determines the task list

attributes.name: processID
attributes.notes: Process triggered by the selected Event

attributes.name: tasks
attributes.notes: Tasks matching this event and product scope

attributes.name: roles
attributes.notes: Roles derived from Tasks in the triggered Process

attributes.name: estimatedHours
attributes.notes: Total execution hours across all Tasks

attributes.name: region
attributes.notes: Region of the factory that owns the parent forecast (via forecastID → Factories.region). Used by Report-B.

Report-A.rule: estimatedHours grouped by productScopeID (scope name), stacked by roles

Report-B.rule: Count of Forecast Scopes grouped by region (from parent Forecast's factory: forecastID → Factories.region)

Tasks.description: A Task is the application of an action to a process activity (workflow). It serves only as an execution template; concrete Jobs are created from Tasks in the Workload module (where taskOwner, taskStatus, taskDueDate and projectID are set).

attributes.name: processID
attributes.notes: Options are processes triggered by the selected event

attributes.name: actionID
attributes.rule: computed: lookup Actions via activityID (display: actionName)

attributes.name: executionTime
attributes.notes: Planned execution time in hours; defaults to Activity.executionTime

Report-A.rule: Count of Tasks grouped by processID

Report-B.rule: Gap between task executionTime and job realExecutionTime, top 10 most critical, sorted ascending by gap

Events.description: An Event is an aggregator of processes; it is not executed itself but triggers a defined execution. Forecasts group which events and execution parameters (forecastScopes) they contain. Corrective-action and nonconformity linkage is deferred to a future module.

attributes.name: sourceID
attributes.notes: Origin context of the event (nullable). ISO §4.1. Enables Sources reports (events by category, source→event→risk chain).

attributes.name: tasks
attributes.notes: Tasks existing for each process triggered by this event

attributes.name: forecastScopes
attributes.notes: Forecast Scopes that use this event to estimate workload

Report-A.rule: Open ticket count (from ticketID rollup) grouped by eventName, per factory

Processes.description: The Process table groups workflows. The user creates a process item that is later used to build the process steps (workflow) records.

attributes.name: processSystemID
attributes.notes: Optional external registry system ID reference; not mandatory

attributes.name: targetExecutionTime
attributes.notes: Average execution time across task groups sharing the same action + workflow combination

attributes.name: activities
attributes.notes: All Activities belonging to this Process

attributes.name: tasks
attributes.notes: All Tasks belonging to this Process

attributes.name: productName
attributes.notes: From tasks rollup, for report filtering

attributes.name: scopeName
attributes.notes: From tasks rollup, for report filtering

Report-A.rule: Count of Tasks grouped by processName

Activities.description: Sub-step within a Process (ISO §4.4.1 / §7.1.6). Activities have no execution time, owner, status, start or end date — execution is defined in the Tasks list. roleID is derived from Tasks.

attributes.name: roleID
attributes.notes: Role is defined at the task level

attributes.name: procedureID
attributes.notes: Allows a link to the document plus a description that is the procedure ID (ISO §7.5)

attributes.name: tasks
attributes.notes: All Tasks derived from this Activity

Workflows.description: Registry of the steps of each process. Through the relations of its records users understand how a process should be executed in terms of activities.

attributes.name: taskID
attributes.notes: Tasks that use this workflow item

Actions.description: An action defines the procedure to be performed to execute a given step (workflow) within a process.

attributes.name: riskID
attributes.notes: Risk this action addresses (nullable). ISO §6.1.2(a) — direct risk→action traceability. Enables Risks/Requirements 'linked action count' reports.

attributes.name: activityID
attributes.notes: From taskID rollup; allows filtering by activity

attributes.name: workflowID
attributes.notes: From taskID rollup; allows filtering by workflow (activity + process)

Constraints.description: Regulatory, design, or operational constraint bounding available risk treatment options (ISO §4.3).

Handouts.description: Input or output document/deliverable exchanged between process steps (ISO §4.4.2(b) — retained documented information).

attributes.name: taskID
attributes.notes: All tasks that have the constraint assigned to it

attributes.name: Template
attributes.notes: Includes a field option to insert a title for the attachment

Channels.description: Communication or delivery channel used in process steps, with assigned owner (ISO §7.4).

attributes.name: channelOwner
attributes.notes: Company or person responsible for managing the channel

attributes.name: handouts
attributes.notes: All Handouts delivered through this channel

Product Scopes.description: Combination of product and scope used for planning and forecasting execution demand (ISO §4.3 / §8.1).

attributes.name: productScopeID
attributes.notes: Displayed as a group code, e.g. "AA"

Scopes.description: Defines the work scope boundaries applicable to products (e.g., Uprating, Temperature Reduction, Redesign) — ISO §4.3.

Products.description: Power transformer products and services tracked through quality and forecasting cycles (ISO §8.1).

Product Class.description: Technical classification of products by voltage rating and power rating ranges (ISO §8.1).

Product Groups.description: Higher-level commercial grouping of product classes for planning (ISO §8.1).

Tickets.description: A Ticket is a project-linked work item capturing the execution context of a project's engineering process. It is the entry point for engineering jobs; Customers, Planners and Managers can create tickets (ISO §10.2).

attributes.name: forecastScopeID
attributes.rule: rollup → Customers (via: customerName, eventID) (display: CONCAT(scopeName, businessSegment, productClassID, productName))
attributes.notes: Forecast Scope this ticket contributes to

attributes.name: targetDate
attributes.notes: When the ticket should be resolved

attributes.name: resolutionTime
attributes.notes: Planned delivery date of the ticket

attributes.name: jobs
attributes.notes: All Jobs associated with this ticket

Report-A.rule: Count of Tickets grouped by customerName

Report-B.rule: Count of tickets grouped by scopeName

Projects.description: Engineering project with customer/factory, owner and status. Groups tickets and provides estimated vs. executed time roll-ups (ISO §8.3 / §8.4).

attributes.name: ticketID
attributes.notes: All tickets belonging to this project

Report-A.rule: Estimated (tasks) vs real (jobs) execution time per project

Jobs.description: A Job is a concrete execution record assigning a person/role to a Ticket to perform a piece of work, tracking planned and actual dates, dependencies and status. Jobs are the primary source of actual usage data for the Control module (ISO §7.1.2).

attributes.name: userID
attributes.notes: Filtered by the roleID of the selected task template

attributes.name: customerName
attributes.rule: computed: from projectID related to ticketID

attributes.name: squadName
attributes.rule: computed: from People (squad) related to userID

Report-A.rule: Count of Jobs reaching jobStatus = Done plotted by week

Capacity.description: Query view (Control module) — no new-item form. Aggregates role/department capacity against allocation over a planning period (ISO §7.1.2 / §6.2).

attributes.name: functionName
attributes.notes: Function of the role (via roleID → Roles.functionID). Used by Report-A/B filters.

attributes.name: availableHours
attributes.notes: Total available hours for the selected roleID in the selected period

attributes.name: allocatedHours
attributes.rule: computed: executionTime of Tasks linked to forecastScopes of each forecastID within a period

Report-A.rule: availableHours vs. allocatedHours side by side grouped by roleName

Usage.description: Query view (Control module) — no new-item form. Tracks actual resource consumption from Jobs against planned Forecast Scope hours (ISO §9.1).

attributes.name: functionName
attributes.notes: Function of the people whose Jobs feed this usage row. Used by Report-A/B filters and series.

attributes.name: customerName
attributes.notes: Customer factory of the Jobs feeding this usage row. Used by Report-A/B filters.

attributes.name: squadName
attributes.notes: Squad of the people whose Jobs feed this usage row. Used by Report-A/B filters.

Report-A.rule: usedHours plotted by period, one series per functionName

Report-B.rule: Heatmap of the most-used roleName by number of allocated jobs

Productivity.description: Query view (Control module) — no new-item form. Efficiency KPIs comparing output vs. target per team (ISO §9.1.1).

Report-A.rule: Count of teams grouped by efficiency bucket (<80% / 80–100% / >100%)

Report-C.rule: Quantity of Tasks that could be done by less experienced resources (by levelRank)

Report-D.rule: Marginal productivity gain of the chosen process or event when adding an extra quantity of a given roleName

Squads.description: Squads group People so that a manager can simulate forecastScopes by squad.

Roles.description: Organisational role with function, skill level and required education (ISO §5.3). Resolves all *Owner foreign keys in the model.

attributes.name: functionID
attributes.notes: Functional area this role belongs to

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.notes: Number of headcount allocated to this role

Report-A.rule: Sum of quantity grouped by functionID (headcount distribution by function)

Report-B.rule: Count of tasks grouped by roleName where headCount > 0

Skill Levels.description: Proficiency level descriptor for a competence or function (ISO §7.2).

Report-A.rule: SUM(Roles.quantity) grouped by levelName

Functions.description: Job function defining the functional scope of work a role performs (ISO §7.2).

attributes.name: functionName
attributes.notes: e.g., Electrical Design, Mechanical Design, Analyst

Report-A.rule: SUM(Roles.quantity) grouped by functionName

Graduation.description: Qualification or education level required or held by a resource (ISO §7.2).

Report-A.rule: SUM(Roles.quantity) grouped by field

Competence.description: Competence profile mapping skills to roles, scopes and products (ISO §7.2), derived largely from the Tasks a role performs.

attributes.name: resources
attributes.rule: computed: list of channelName of every input and output from Tasks via taskID

Report-A.rule: Matrix of Role (rows) × Scope (columns); cell = % of required competences that are isCertified

People.description: Individual users and team members (ISO §5.3 / §7.1.2). All *Owner foreign keys across the model resolve to a Person.

attributes.name: roleID
attributes.notes: Role held by this person. Resolves Roles.people rollup, Roles.overheadCost, and the Jobs assignee-by-role filter. Consistent with functionID (role's function).

Report-A.rule: Count of active People grouped by functionID

Onboarding.description: Tracks a person's certification progress against the competences derived from their function and roles (ISO §7.2).

attributes.name: constraintName
attributes.rule: computed: Competence via roleID (display: FOREACH([{constrainTypeName: constraintName}]))

Risks.description: Risk and opportunity register with severity/likelihood assessment and RPN (ISO §6.1.1).

Report-A.rule: X-axis = riskLikelihood, Y-axis = riskSeverity, bubble size = riskPriorityNumber, colour = riskCategory; quadrant lines at 3/3

Report-B.query: Risks where riskStatus IN (Open, UnderTreatment); columns: title, category, severity, likelihood, RPN, owner, status, linked action count, last reviewed

Sources.description: External and internal issue sources feeding events and requirements (ISO §4.1).

attributes.name: requirements
attributes.notes: Requirements that originate from this source

Report-A.rule: Count of Events grouped by sourceCategoryID

Report-B.query: Sources with event count, risk count, open risk count; traceable chain from source to event to risk

Source Categories.description: Lookup classification of issue sources (internal, customer, regulatory, etc.). Used as a filter dimension in Sources and Events reports (ISO §4.1).

Requirements.description: Needs and expectations of interested parties mapped to sources, products and risks (ISO §4.2).

Report-A.rule: Count of Requirements grouped by requirementType

Report-B.query: Requirements joined with Risks; columns: requirement name, type, source, linked risk count, open risk count, highest RPN

actionApplication.description: Lookup table classifying quality management actions (Risk Management, Control, Communication, Monitoring, Improvement). Used as a filter dimension in Actions reports (ISO §6.1.2(b)).

Event Log.description: Audit trail of event state changes for traceability (ISO §9.1.1 / §10.2).

Report-A.rule: Count of resolved Events grouped by resolution time buckets (<1h / 1–8h / 8–24h / 1–7d / >7d)

Report-B.query: Full state-change history for a selected event; columns: previous status, new status, changed by, changed at, note
